fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The ICC Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England, won by the West Indies, who defeated Australia in the final at Lord's.
India won the ICC Cricket World Cup in 2011 on home soil, defeating Sri Lanka in the final at the Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for the most international runs in cricket history, scoring over 34,000 runs across Tests and ODIs combined.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
Lionel Messi won his first FIFA World Cup title in 2022 when Argentina defeated France on penalties after a 3-3 draw in Qatar.
The UEFA Champions League is the most prestigious annual club football competition in Europe, first held in the 1955-56 season as the European Cup.
The four Grand Slam tournaments in tennis are the Australian Open, the French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, and the US Open.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in the Open Era, surpassing Rafael Nadal and Roger Federer.
Serena Williams won 23 Grand Slam singles titles during her career, the most by any player in the Open Era.
Wimbledon, first held in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and the only Grand Slam still played on grass courts.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
The Badminton World Federation (BWF) governs international badminton and organizes the BWF World Championships along with the Thomas and Uber Cups.
PV Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win an Olympic silver medal in badminton at the 2016 Rio Olympics.
The NBA (National Basketball Association) was founded in 1946 and is the premier professional basketball league in the world.
The Boston Celtics hold the record for the most NBA championships won, with 18 titles as of the 2023-24 season.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls in the 1990s and is widely regarded as one of the greatest players in basketball history.
The FIBA Basketball World Cup is the sport's premier international competition for national teams, first held in 1950.
